---
title: "Earthquake Events"
---

## Dataset Reference

<table class="data-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Dataset</th>
      <th>Measurement</th>
      <th>Units</th>
      <th>Source</th>
      <th>Release Year</th>
      <th>Years of Data</th>
      <th>Coverage</th>
      <th>Resolution</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td>Forest And Buildings removed Copernicus DEM </td>
      <td>Elevation</td>
      <td>Meters above sea level</td>
      <td><a href="https://data.bris.ac.uk/data/dataset/s5hqmjcdj8yo2ibzi9b4ew3sn" target="_blank">Fathom</a></td>
      <td>2023</td>
      <td>NA</td>
      <td>Global</td>
      <td>30 meters</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

---


::: {.wiki-section}
## Source Data Citation

<div class="citation-block">
National Geophysical Data Center / World Data Service (NGDC/WDS): NCEI/WDS Global Significant Earthquake Database. NOAA National Centers for Environmental Information. DOI:10.7289/V5TD9V7K 

Note that the cited damage level is for the earthquake overall, not necessarily in the city of interest. The database defines damage levels by monetary cost: limited, < $1 million; moderate, $1–5 million; severe, $5–24 million; extreme, $25+ million. 
</div>
:::
